title: fragariastrum valderium
intro: Fragariastrum valderium, also known as Valerian root, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the Valerianaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Valerian root has been found to possess sedative and antispasmodic properties, making it a popular remedy for anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. Its medicinal properties and health benefits include reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality, as well as alleviating menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. Valerian root can be prepared into teas, tinctures, capsules, or dried root forms for consumption, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
